title: Generate All Subsets (Power Set)
description: Write a recursive function that generates all possible subsets of a given array. This is also known as the power set problem and is a classic example of recursive problem-solving.
instructions: Create a function named `generateSubsets` that accepts an array of numbers
Return an array of all possible subsets (including the empty set)
Use recursion to build the subsets
Each subset should be a unique combination of elements
The order of subsets doesn't matter

hints.content: Use backtracking: at each step, you have two choices for each element - include it or exclude it from the current subset. This creates a decision tree.
Create a helper function that takes the current position and current subset. Add the current subset to results, then recursively try adding each remaining element.
Pattern: result.push([...current]); then for each element from start to end: add element, recurse with next index, remove element (backtrack). Start with backtrack(0, []).
